title: POCUS-CARE
text:
Point-of-Care Ultrasound for Conflict-Affected Settings (POCUS-CARE) is an international educational initiative dedicated to improving access to high-quality point-of-care ultrasound (POCUS) education for healthcare professionals working in conflict-affected and resource-limited settings.
📖 Read the textbook
🎓 Available training sessions

title: About
text:
Pierre Catoire, MD, MSc
Emergency Doctor, Vice-President of Mehad
Pierre Catoire developed the POCUS-CARE curriculum and authored the open-access textbook, with the objective of providing a structured, evidence-based ultrasound training programme adapted to conflict-affected settings.

title: POCUS-CARE course website
text:
An open access POCUS initiative
POCUS-CARE intends to develop use of ultrasound in conflict contexts, where its use has the most impact on diagnosis and early decision making. The training program is based on the following principles:

Action-oriented – Learning is organised around clinical questions that directly support diagnosis, triage, treatment, and referral decisions.
Context-adapted – While the educational framework is shared across countries, each national implementation is adapted to local epidemiology, healthcare systems, available resources, and national priorities.
Evidence-based – Educational content is developed by certified POCUS instructors and follows current international evidence and clinical guidelines. References are provided throughout the course.
Collaborative – National instructors lead the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of the program within their own countries.
Freely available – Educational materials are distributed under the licence described below, allowing non-commercial reuse and adaptation. In-person sessions are free, without any fee for the learners.

title: POCUS-CARE
text:
Learning outcomes
After completing the programme, participants will be able to:

Perform focused ultrasound examinations using a systematic scanning approach.
Identify common life-threatening conditions, including intra-abdominal haemorrhage, cardiac tamponade, and pneumothorax.
Use ultrasound findings to support the assessment of circulatory and respiratory failure.
Identify selected musculoskeletal conditions, including soft-tissue foreign bodies and long-bone diaphyseal fractures.
Diagnose deep vein thrombosis using focused ultrasound.
Perform ultrasound-guided vascular access.
Integrate ultrasound findings into clinical decision-making, while recognising the limitations of point-of-care ultrasound and the potential for false-positive and false-negative findings.

For a detailed description of the curriculum, visit the online textbook.

title: POCUS-CARE
text:
Licence

Original content
Unless otherwise stated, the original educational material published on this website and in the textbook is distributed under the Creative Commons Attribution–NonCommercial–ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) licence.
You are free to:

Share — copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format.
Adapt — remix, transform, and build upon the material.

Under the following conditions:

Attribution — Appropriate credit must be given, a link to the licence provided, and any modifications indicated.
NonCommercial — The material may not be used for commercial purposes.
ShareAlike — Adaptations must be distributed under the same licence.
No additional restrictions — No legal or technological restrictions may be applied beyond those permitted by the licence.








Third-party content
Unless otherwise indicated, all educational content is original. Third-party material remains subject to its own copyright and licence terms.
We gratefully acknowledge educational resources made available by The POCUS Atlas, which have contributed to the development of this programme.

title: POCUS-CARE
text:
An open-access educational initiative
POCUS-CARE aims to expand access to high-quality ultrasound education in conflict-affected settings, where POCUS can have a major impact on diagnosis, triage, and early clinical decision-making.
The programme is based on the following educational principles:

Action-oriented — Learning is organised around clinical questions that directly support diagnosis, triage, treatment, and referral decisions.
Context-adapted — While the educational framework is shared across countries, each national implementation is adapted to local epidemiology, healthcare systems, available resources, and national priorities.
Evidence-based — Educational content is developed by certified POCUS instructors and follows current international evidence and clinical guidelines. References are provided throughout the learning resources.
Collaborative — National instructors lead the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of the programme within their own countries.
Freely available — Educational resources are distributed under the licence described below, allowing non-commercial reuse and adaptation. In-person training is provided free of charge for participants.

🏫 Become an instructor

title: About

Pierre Catoire, MD, MSc
Emergency Doctor, Vice-President of Mehad
Pierre Catoire developed the POCUS-CARE curriculum and authored the open-access textbook, with the objective of providing a structured, evidence-based ultrasound training programme adapted to conflict-affected settings.

title: About
text:
Course Directors






Mehdi El Melali, MD
Emergency Doctor, Board Member of Mehad
Course directors oversee curriculum development and implementation, educational quality, instructor training, and continuous programme improvement.

title: About
text:
Supporting Organisations
The POCUS-CARE initiative is supported by partner organisations involved in humanitarian health, medical education, and capacity building. Current supporting organisations include:

Mehad: implementation of the program in Ukraine, Palestine and Lebanon
MedGlobal: implementation of the program in Lebanon
ANERA: implementation of the program in Lebanon

title: Resources
text: Many high-quality educational resources are freely available online. The following websites, image libraries, and video channels provide complementary material for learners wishing to deepen their knowledge of point-of-care ultrasound.

title: Resources
text:
Image atlases

The POCUS Atlas : A free educational atlas containing hundreds of annotated ultrasound images and videos covering emergency medicine, critical care, internal medicine, and musculoskeletal ultrasound.
Radiopaedia: An extensive open-access radiology reference, including numerous articles, ultrasound cases, and image collections.

title: Resources
text:
Educational websites

ACEP Sonoguide: One of the most comprehensive online references for emergency ultrasound, developed by the American College of Emergency Physicians.
Society of Point of Care Ultrasound (SPOCUS): Educational resources, guidelines, webinars, and learning material dedicated to point-of-care ultrasound.
WINFOCUS: The World Interactive Network Focused on Critical Ultrasound provides educational material, international recommendations, and information on POCUS education worldwide.
The Ultrasound Journal: Open-access scientific journal publishing clinical and educational articles on point-of-care ultrasound.

title: Resources
text:
YouTube channels

123 Sonography: High-quality lectures and practical demonstrations covering a wide range of ultrasound applications.
5 Minute Sono: Short educational videos focusing on practical bedside ultrasound techniques.
The POCUS Atlas: Educational cases, image interpretation, and practical scanning demonstrations.
Core Ultrasound: Educational videos produced by emergency physicians covering image acquisition and interpretation.

title: Resources
text:
Clinical guidelines
Learners are encouraged to consult national and international clinical practice guidelines alongside the POCUS-CARE textbook. Whenever possible, educational content within the textbook is based on current evidence and recommendations from international scientific societies.

title: Contact
text: General questions and requests may be addressed at: training.programs@mehad.fr

title: Training Sessions
text:
Training programmes
POCUS-CARE currently offers two educational programmes.

POCUS-CARE Provider
The Provider course is intended for healthcare professionals wishing to acquire the essential knowledge and practical skills required to safely perform point-of-care ultrasound in emergency and acute care.
➡️ Learn more about the Provider course


POCUS-CARE Instructor
The Instructor programme prepares experienced POCUS practitioners to become certified POCUS-CARE instructors capable of teaching, mentoring, and supporting future courses within their own countries.
➡️ Learn more about the Instructor course

title: Training Sessions

Registration
Registration is organised locally in collaboration with national Ministries of Health and partner organisations.
For further information, please contact training.programs@mehad.fr

title: Provider Course
text:
The POCUS-CARE Provider Course is designed to teach the core knowledge and practical skills required to safely perform point-of-care ultrasound (POCUS) in emergency and acute care settings.
The programme combines self-directed learning using the open-access POCUS-CARE textbook with instructor-led lectures, supervised hands-on practice, and competency-based assessment.

title: Provider Course
text:
The POCUS-CARE Provider Course is designed to teach the core knowledge and practical skills required to safely perform point-of-care ultrasound (POCUS) in emergency and acute care settings.
The programme combines self-directed learning using the open-access POCUS-CARE textbook with instructor-led lectures, supervised hands-on practice, and competency-based assessment.

title: Provider Course
text:
Course objectives
At the end of the course, participants will be able to:

Perform focused ultrasound examinations using a systematic scanning approach.
Acquire standard cardiac, lung, abdominal and vascular ultrasound views.
Recognise common normal and pathological ultrasound findings.
Integrate ultrasound findings into clinical decision-making.
Perform selected ultrasound-guided procedures.
Recognise the limitations of point-of-care ultrasound.

title: Provider Course
text:
Target audience
The course is intended for healthcare professionals authorised to perform point-of-care ultrasound within their country of practice.
No previous ultrasound experience is required.

title: Provider Course
text:
Prerequisites
Participants should:

Be licensed healthcare professionals (according to national regulations).
Work in emergency, critical care, primary care, anaesthesia, surgery, or another clinical discipline where POCUS is relevant.

title: Provider Course
text:
Course format
The course is implemented in three phases:

phase 1: 2-day in-person training session combining interactive lectures with hands-on practical workshops
phase 2: Clinical mentorship in the learners’ hospital departments
phase 3: Wrap-up and assessment

A refresher session is conducted 6 to 12 weeks, discussing cases encountered during the first months of practice.

title: Provider Course
text:
Course schedule
The Provider Course is typically delivered over three days.







Day
Main topics




Day 1
Ultrasound physics, device settings, orientation, abdominal ultrasound


Day 2
Cardiac ultrasound, lung ultrasound


Day 3
Vascular ultrasound, bones and soft tissues, ultrasound-guided procedures, assessments



(The schedule may be adapted according to local needs.)

title: Provider Course
text:
Assessment
Certification of program completion requires:

Completion of the pre-test
Full attendance to all phases
Completion of at least 10 supervised ultrasound exams during the mentorship phase, demonstrated in the provided logbook
Successful completion of the post-training written exam
A practical skills assessment, demonstrating ability to perform an E-FAST exam within 5 minutes

Participants who successfully complete all assessments receive the POCUS-CARE Provider Certificate.
The certificate confirms successful completion of the educational programme. It does not constitute a licence or legal authorisation to independently perform ultrasound examinations.

title: Provider Course
text:
Certification
Participants who successfully complete all assessments receive the POCUS-CARE Provider Certificate.
The certificate confirms successful completion of the educational programme. It does not constitute a licence or legal authorisation to independently perform ultrasound examinations.

title: Provider Course
text:
Educational resources
Participants receive free access to the POCUS-CARE online textbook and additional educational resources before, during, and after the course.

title: Provider Course
text:
Upcoming sessions
Visit the Course Overview page for announcements about future sessions.

title: Instructor Course
text:
The POCUS-CARE Instructor Course prepares experienced POCUS practitioners to teach within the POCUS-CARE educational programme.
The course focuses on educational methods, practical teaching skills, learner assessment, and quality assurance in addition to advanced ultrasound instruction.

title: Instructor Course
text:
The POCUS-CARE Instructor Course prepares experienced POCUS practitioners to teach within the POCUS-CARE educational programme.
The course focuses on educational methods, practical teaching skills, learner assessment, and quality assurance in addition to advanced ultrasound instruction.

title: Instructor Course
text:
Course objectives
After completing the programme, participants will be able to:

Teach the POCUS-CARE curriculum using standardised educational methods.
Demonstrate effective bedside teaching and practical instruction.
Supervise hands-on ultrasound workshops.
Assess learner performance using structured assessment tools.
Deliver constructive feedback.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of the national POCUS-CARE programme.

title: Instructor Course
text:
Target audience
Healthcare professionals with previous experience in point-of-care ultrasound who wish to become certified POCUS-CARE instructors.

title: Instructor Course
text:
Prerequisites
Applicants should:

Hold a POCUS-CARE Provider Certificate (or demonstrate equivalent competence).
Have regular clinical experience using point-of-care ultrasound.
Demonstrate motivation for medical education.
Be recommended by their local training programme whenever applicable.

title: Instructor Course
text:
Course format
The Instructor Course includes:

Interactive educational workshops
Teaching methodology
Practical teaching sessions
Supervised instruction
Peer feedback
Educational discussions

title: Instructor Course
text:
Course schedule
The Instructor Course is typically delivered over two days.







Day
Main topics




Day 1
Educational principles, teaching methodology, practical demonstrations


Day 2
Supervised teaching, learner assessment, feedback, instructor evaluation



(The schedule may be adapted according to local needs.)

title: Instructor Course
text:
Assessment
Candidates are evaluated on their ability to:

Demonstrate sound ultrasound knowledge.
Teach practical ultrasound skills.
Facilitate small-group learning.
Provide effective learner feedback.
Apply the POCUS-CARE educational principles.

title: Instructor Course
text:
Certification
Candidates who successfully complete all assessments receive the POCUS-CARE Instructor Certificate.
Certification recognises competence as a POCUS-CARE instructor within the programme. Ongoing participation in educational activities may be required to maintain instructor status.

title: Instructor Course
text:
Instructor responsibilities
Certified instructors are expected to:

Teach according to the POCUS-CARE curriculum.
Maintain high educational standards.
Participate in quality improvement activities.
Support the development of future instructors.
Contribute to the sustainability of national training programmes.

title: Instructor Course
text:
Instructor Journey

Potential Instructors (PIs) are identified during Provider Sessions, by the instructors
PIs and external invited instructors participate in a Training of Trainers (ToT) Session
Obtention of the POCUS-CARE Instructor Course Completion Certificate grants the status of Junior Instructor (JI)
Junior Instructors participate in instruction during Provider courses, alongside a Senior Instructor
Junior Instructors may apply to the status of Senior Instructor after participation at at least two Provider courses as instructor

title: Instructor Course
text:
Upcoming sessions
Visit the Course Overview page for announcements about future sessions.
